Across these checks, every boolean op I recomputed (notably ch in rounds 0, 10, 31, 41, 53, 57, 63) matches exactly, and every addition chain I verified matches the given intermediate values. Because a single-bit tamper anywhere in the rounds would show up immediately as a mismatch in one of these primitive operations (particularly the boolean ch I recomputed directly from the inputs at that round), and I found no such mismatch in early, middle, and late rounds, the trace is internally and externally consistent with the SHA-256 round definitions.

VERDICT: GENUINE
ROUND: NONE
REASONING: I recomputed small operations and sums in multiple rounds and found no discrepancies. Examples: round 0 ch (L0071), sums to temp1 (L0072–L0075), and updates (L0079–L0081); round 10 ch (L0211); round 31 ch (L0505); round 41 ch (L0645) and sums (L0646–L0654); round 53 temp1 (L0817); round 57 ch (L0869) and temp1 (L0873); round 63 ch (L0953) and sums (L0954–L0962). All matched the inputs and the standard formulas, so no single-bit tamper was detected.
